title: uragoga mannii
intro: Uragoga mannii, commonly known as African pepper, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga mannii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as analgesic and antispasmodic effects. Additionally, its leaves and roots have been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
side_effects: Uragoga mannii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, as well as headaches and dizziness due to its potential interaction with blood pressure medications. In excessive amounts, it may also cause kidney damage and liver toxicity. Additionally, uragoga mannii has been known to exacerbate certain medical conditions, such as high blood pressure and kidney disease. Misuse of this plant can lead to a range of health complications, making it essential to consult a medical professional before using it.
